summary: Replayed ADR 0181 from the merged `main` branch, reran the docker-runtime-lv3 control-plane backup, mirrored the refreshed controller recovery bundle to backup-lv3, passed the immediate restore drill, and published plus re-verified the off-host control metadata witness generation `20260327T103801Z-dca744432518`.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest pytest -q tests/test_control_metadata_witness.py` passed after adding the shard-root regression that exercises witness sync from `.ansible/shards`.

`make converge-control-plane-recovery PLATFORM_TRACE_ID=adr0181main2` completed successfully from merged `main` commit `dca74443`, including the runtime backup rerun, controller bundle mirror, restore drill, witness publication, and witness verification.
The immediate `lv3-control-plane-backup` replay on `docker-runtime-lv3` completed successfully at `2026-03-27T10:37:13Z`, refreshing the latest archived runtime recovery set on `backup-lv3`.
The backup-store restore drill replay completed successfully and its state file was re-read successfully at `2026-03-27T10:37:39Z` during the merged-main replay.

A top-level witness sync from merged `main` wrote `receipts/witness-replication/20260327T103801Z-dca744432518-control-metadata-witness.json`, and `python3 scripts/control_metadata_witness.py verify --archive-root /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/control-metadata-witness/archive` confirmed the latest generation `20260327T103801Z-dca744432518`.

notes: The first merged-main replay attempt (`adr0181main`) stopped early because the local OpenBao health endpoint on docker-runtime-lv3 briefly refused the seal-status probe; the immediate retry (`adr0181main2`) completed successfully without further code changes.

This receipt supersedes the older ADR 0051 control-plane recovery live-apply receipt for the control-plane recovery capability because the replay now includes off-host witness publication and verification as part of the same governed workflow.
